challenges.title: Progress Bar
challenges.description: Create a progress element with a value attribute and a max attribute to show task completion. Add a label element associated with the progress bar. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a labeled progress bar -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '<progress' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <progress> element'
assert 'value' in html.lower(), 'Progress should have a value attribute'
assert 'max' in html.lower(), 'Progress should have a max attribute'
assert '</progress>' in html.lower(), 'Should close the <progress> element'
assert '<label' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <label> element'
hints: The <progress> element uses value and max attributes, e.g. value="70" max="100".
Use a <label> with a for attribute matching the progress element's id.

challenges.title: Multiple Progress
challenges.description: Create three progress bars showing different completion levels (e.g., 25%, 50%, 75%). Each progress bar should have a label describing what it tracks. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create three labeled progress bars at different levels -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
lower = html.lower()
assert lower.count('<progress') >= 3, 'Should contain at least 3 <progress> elements'
assert lower.count('</progress>') >= 3, 'Should close all <progress> elements'
assert lower.count('<label') >= 3, 'Should contain at least 3 <label> elements'
assert 'value' in lower, 'Progress elements should have value attributes'
assert 'max' in lower, 'Progress elements should have max attributes'
hints: Create three separate <progress> elements, each with different value attributes.
Give each progress element a unique id so you can associate it with its label.
For 25% completion with max="100", use value="25".
